root_cause: HPA 'api-hpa' is conflicting with manual or GitOps replica changes on Deployment/api
evidence: HPA 'api-hpa' targets Deployment/api and rescaled it as: 6 -> 6
Deployment/api managedFields show 2 recent external replica write(s) by kubectl-scale
ReplicaSet 'api-6dbf78c7f9' was alternately scaled as: up to 6 -> down to 2 -> up to 6 -> down to 2
Deployment 'api' currently asks for 2 replicas while HPA status still wants 6
Scale ownership conflict lasted 14.0 minutes with 3 controller scale reversals
Deployment 'api' still reports only 1/2 available replicas and 1/2 updated replicas

causes.message: HPA 'api-hpa' and external scale writers both updated Deployment/api during the same incident window
HorizontalPodAutoscaler is fighting with manual or GitOps replica changes on the same workload
External manager(s) kubectl-scale kept writing the replica count after HPA reconciliation
Deployment availability remains unstable at 1/2 while competing scale writers keep changing desired size

pod:api-6dbf78c7f9-zr8kd: Pod belongs to HPA-managed workload Deployment/api

likely_causes: Someone used `kubectl scale` or patched replicas directly while the HPA was still active
A GitOps or delivery controller is continuously reconciling `spec.replicas` on a workload that is also managed by HPA
An emergency manual scale change was left in place instead of disabling or removing the HPA first
Multiple automation systems are writing the scale subresource and Deployment spec at the same time
suggested_checks: kubectl get deployment api -n default -o yaml --show-managed-fields
Remove `spec.replicas` from GitOps-managed manifests for HPA-controlled workloads or suspend the HPA before manual scaling
Audit recent scale or patch commands and delivery-controller reconciliations during the incident window
